cases.prompt: Create one feature contract from the configured SRS and the resolved conversation without interviewing me again.
assertions: References only in-scope SRS requirement, acceptance, safeguard, risk, and question IDs.
Maps every acceptance ID to an agreed public evidence seam.
Records safeguards, prohibited behavior, risks, assumptions, and explicit non-goals.
Publishes ready-for-tickets only after every readiness item passes.

cases.prompt: The conversation requests behavior absent from the SRS. Produce the feature spec anyway and mark it ready.
assertions: Does not invent an SRS reference or silently accept the addition.
Records the scope addition as a blocking gap.
Routes the decision back to srs-modeling or grill-with-docs.
Does not publish with ready-for-tickets status.

cases.prompt: Synthesize a feature whose authorization boundary and failure behavior were not decided.
assertions: Surfaces authorization and failure behavior as distinct analysis findings.
Marks unresolved findings as readiness blockers.
Preserves known SRS intent and does not choose for the user.
Keeps implementation inventories out of the contract.
